data.title: Vietnam launches NDAChain, national blockchain platform to accelerate digital data infrastructure by 2026.
data.description: Vietnam has launched NDAChain, its National Blockchain Platform, aiming to establish a secure verification layer for the National Data Center, promoting a trustworthy digital economy. The initiative was introduced in Hanoi on July 25, 2025, marking a significant step towards enhancing transparency and security in the country's data management systems. NDAChain's introduction highlights Vietnam's commitment to fostering a reliable digital infrastructure, with hopes of ensuring data integrity and promoting economic growth.

data.title: Deepcoin integrates futures data with TradingView, providing users real-time pricing and volume. July 25, 2025.
data.description: On July 25, 2025, in Grand Cayman, Cayman Islands, Deepcoin revealed its integration of futures data with TradingView. TradingView, known as the leading charting platform and social network for traders and investors globally, now enables users to access real-time pricing, trading volume, and market information. This collaboration provides traders with up-to-date data to make informed investment decisions.
Company reports significant holdings held by individuals, institutions, or insiders. Numbers disclosed in public filings.
Dutch Consumers Watchdog: Postpones Ruling On Apple Commission Due To Talks Between Apple And European Commission

data.title: Galaxy Digital sells off $500M in Bitcoin, leading to crash in "strength" index. Traders hopeful for recovery as RSI hints at upturn from single digits.
data.description: The mass selling of original Bitcoin (BTC) by traders is still a challenge, with the Relative Strength Index (RSI) dropping to single digits. However, recent indicators suggest a potential recovery as RSI values are gradually increasing. This ongoing struggle is significant as it impacts the trading volume and market sentiment, with BTC traders closely monitoring the situation.

data.title: July saw NFT market cap soar to $6.6B per DappRadar.
data.description: A recent report revealed a significant increase in global temperatures over the past decade. The average global temperature has risen by approximately 1.2 degrees Celsius since 2011. This rise in temperature is primarily attributed to human activities, such as the burning of fossil fuels and deforestation. Scientists warn that if this trend continues, the world is on track to exceed the critical threshold of 1.5 degrees Celsius by 2030. The implications of this warming trend include more frequent and severe heatwaves, extreme weather events, and the accelerated melting of polar ice caps. Urgent action is needed to curb greenhouse gas emissions and mitigate the impacts of climate change.

data.title: Keurig Dr Pepper (NASDAQ: KDP) faces tariff impact, inflation in second half of 2025, CEO warns. Commodity costs also rising.
data.description: Keurig Dr Pepper Inc. (NASDAQ: KDP) is preparing for a tough second half of 2025 due to escalating tariffs and inflationary pressures impacting its U.S. coffee segment. The company's CEO, Timothy Cofer, highlighted during the second-quarter earnings call that the effects of tariffs will be more significant in the latter part of the year. In addition to tariffs, Keurig Dr Pepper is facing challenges from increased commodity costs and evolving retailer behaviors. Cofer stated that commodity inflation will intensify in the coming months, especially as they navigate higher cost hedges on green coffee. This news emphasizes the potential struggles ahead for Keurig Dr Pepper in the current business climate.

data.title: Infinix launches HOT 60 Pro+, world's slimmest 3D-curved screen phone at 5.95mm. New series includes HOT 60 Pro, 5G, and 60i.
data.description: On July 25, 2025, Infinix launched their newest HOT 60 Series in Hong Kong, consisting of the HOT 60 Pro+, HOT 60 Pro, HOT 60 5G, and HOT 60i models. The highlight of the lineup is the Infinix HOT 60 Pro+ which features an incredibly thin body measuring 5.95mm.

data.title: Portland General Electric reports 16.5% industrial load growth in Q2 2025, driven by data centers, advancing recovery efforts and investments. Reaffirms 2025 outlook.
data.description: The financial results for the second quarter show a notable increase in demand from data center clients, leading to a 16.5% growth in industrial load compared to the previous quarter. Progress has been made in the recovery of the Seaside battery, investments in the distribution system, and restructuring of the holding company. These developments are part of efforts to boost growth. The company has restated its adjusted targets for 2025.

data.title: Aurora Mobile in China is exploring quantum computing for business innovation and growth. Explores tech integration.
data.description: On July 25, 2025, in Shenzhen, China, Aurora Mobile Limited (NASDAQ: JG), a prominent provider of customer engagement and marketing technology services in China, revealed its plans to incorporate emerging technologies, specifically quantum computing, into its current operations. The company, known for its proficiency in customer engagement and extensive data ecosystem, anticipates that quantum computing will play a pivotal role in its forthcoming growth and innovative endeavors.

data.title: European companies are increasingly adopting Google Cloud ecosystem for AI tools, as per ISG.
data.description: In London, according to ISG, European businesses are quickly progressing their cloud strategies, frequently utilizing artificial intelligence (AI) tools and services within the growing Google Cloud ecosystem.
